content:
# Joshua Chapter 9 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 232-233 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_009_page_001.jpg`

---

**9:1** And-it-was when-heard all the-kings who [were] on-[the]-other-side of-the-Jordan, in-the-hill-country and-in-the-lowland and-in-all [the]-coast of-the-Sea the-Great, toward [the]-front of-the-Lebanon -- the-Hittite and-the-Amorite, the-Canaanite, the-Perizzite, the-Hivite, and-the-Jebusite --

**9:2** and-they-gathered-themselves together, to-fight with Joshua and-with Israel, [with] one mouth.

**9:3** And-[the]-inhabitants of-Gibeon heard that which Joshua had-done to-Jericho and-to-the-Ai.

**9:4** And-they also acted with-craftiness, and-they-went and-disguised-themselves, and-they-took sacks worn-out upon their-donkeys, and-wineskins worn-out and-torn and-bound-up,

**9:5** and-sandals worn-out and-patched upon their-feet, and-garments worn-out upon them, and-all [the]-bread of-their-provision [was] dry, [it] had-become crumbs.

**9:6** And-they-went to Joshua, to the-camp, [to] the-Gilgal, and-they-said to him and-to [the]-man of-Israel, "From-a-land far we-have-come; and-now, cut for-us a-covenant."

**9:7** And-said [the]-man of-Israel to the-Hivite, "Perhaps in-my-midst you [are] dwelling, and-how shall-I-cut for-you a-covenant?"

**9:8** And-they-said to Joshua, "Your-servants we [are]." And-said Joshua to them, "Who [are] you, and-from-where do-you-come?"

**9:9** And-they-said to him, "From-a-land very far have-come your-servants, on-account-of-[the]-name of-YHWH your-God, for we-have-heard his-fame, and all that He-did in-Egypt,

**9:10** and all that He-did to-[the]-two kings of-the-Amorite who [were] on-[the]-other-side of-the-Jordan, to-Sihon king of-Heshbon and-to-Og king of-the-Bashan, who [was] in-Ashtaroth.

**9:11** And-said to us our-elders and-all [the]-inhabitants of-our-land, saying, 'Take in-your-hand provision for-the-journey, and-go to-meet them, and-say to them, "Your-servants we [are], and-now cut for-us a-covenant."'

**9:12** This our-bread -- hot we-provisioned-ourselves [with]-it from-our-houses on-[the]-day of-our-going-out to-go to you, and-now, behold, it-has-become-dry and-it-has-become crumbs.

**9:13** And-these [are the]-wineskins of-wine which we-filled new, and-behold, they-have-been-torn. And-these our-garments and-our-sandals have-worn-out from-[the]-greatness of-the-journey, very-much."

**9:14** And-took the-men from-their-provision, and [the]-mouth of-YHWH they-did not ask.

**9:15** And-made for-them Joshua peace, and-he-cut for-them a-covenant to-let-them-live, and-swore to-them [the]-leaders of-the-congregation.

**9:16** And-it-was at-[the]-end of-three days after that they-had-cut for-them a-covenant, and-they-heard that near they [were] to them, and-in-their-midst they [were] dwelling.

**9:17** And-journeyed [the]-sons of-Israel and-they-came to their-cities on-the-day the-third. And-their-cities [were] Gibeon and-the-Chephirah and-Beeroth and-Kiriath-jearim.

**9:18** And-did not strike them [the]-sons of-Israel, for had-sworn to-them [the]-leaders of-the-congregation by-YHWH God of-Israel. And-murmured all the-congregation against the-leaders.

**9:19** And-said all the-leaders to all the-congregation, "We have-sworn to them by-YHWH God of-Israel, and-now we-are not able to-touch them.

**9:20** This we-shall-do to them: and-we-shall-let-them-live, and-there-shall not be upon us wrath on-account-of the-oath which we-swore to them."

**9:21** And-said to them the-leaders, "Let-them-live." And-they-became choppers of-wood and-drawers of-water for-all the-congregation, as had-spoken to them the-leaders.

**9:22** And-called for-them Joshua, and-he-spoke to them, saying, "Why did-you-deceive us, saying, 'Very far we [are] from you,' and-you [are] in-our-midst dwelling?

**9:23** And-now cursed [are] you, and-shall-never be-cut-off from-you a-slave, and-choppers of-wood and-drawers of-water for-[the]-house of-my-God."

**9:24** And-they-answered Joshua and-said, "Because telling it-was-told to-your-servants that YHWH your-God commanded Moses his-servant to-give to-you all the-land, and-to-destroy all [the]-inhabitants of-the-land from-before you, and-we-feared greatly for our-lives from-before you, and-we-did this thing.

**9:25** And-now, behold-us in-your-hand; as-the-good and-as-the-right in-your-eyes to-do to-us, do."

**9:26** And-he-did to-them so, and-he-delivered them from-[the]-hand of-[the]-sons of-Israel, and-they-did not kill them.

**9:27** And-gave them Joshua on-that day choppers of-wood and-drawers of-water for-the-congregation and-for-[the]-altar of-YHWH, unto this day, to the-place which He-would-choose.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **9:2 "one mouth"** (פֶּה אֶחָד) -- Literally "mouth one." An idiom meaning "with one accord" or "unanimously." EMET preserves the literal body-part metaphor.

2. **9:4 "disguised themselves"** (וַיִּצְטַיָּרוּ) -- Hitpael of צור. The root carries the sense of "provisioning" or "equipping as envoys." Some read this as "made themselves as ambassadors" (cf. LXX: ἐπεσιτίσαντο, "provisioned themselves"). The Leningrad Codex reading is retained; the word is rare and debated.

3. **9:5 "had become crumbs"** (הָיָה נִקֻּדִים) -- The term נִקֻּדִים refers to bread that is dried and crumbled, or "spotted" with mold. Literally "dotted/spotted [things]."

4. **9:7 "man of Israel"** (אִישׁ יִשְׂרָאֵל) -- Singular collective, "man of Israel," preserved literally rather than smoothing to "men of Israel."

5. **9:14 "the mouth of YHWH they did not ask"** (וְאֵת פִּי יהוה לֹא שָׁאָלוּ) -- A pivotal theological note: Israel's leaders failed to inquire of YHWH before making the covenant. The "mouth" of YHWH = His declared will or oracle.

6. **9:23 "shall never be cut off from you a slave"** (וְלֹא יִכָּרֵת מִכֶּם עֶבֶד) -- Literally "and-not shall-be-cut-off from-you a-slave." The sense is that servitude shall never depart from the Gibeonites -- there will always be slaves among them.

7. **9:24 "telling it was told"** (הַגֵּד הֻגַּד) -- Infinitive absolute + Hophal finite verb. The doubling construction (infinitive absolute) is preserved per EMET protocol, emphasizing the certainty of the report given to the Gibeonites.

8. **9:27 "to the place which He would choose"** (אֶל הַמָּקוֹם אֲשֶׁר יִבְחָר) -- A forward-looking Deuteronomic formula (cf. Deut 12:5, 11, 14). The imperfect יִבְחָר indicates the place YHWH "will choose" -- Jerusalem is not yet named.

9. **LXX Vaticanus Variants (Codex B, folio 168):** The LXX of Joshua 9 differs significantly in verse numbering and content from the MT. The LXX places the Gibeonite narrative after additional material and uses different verse divisions (LXX 9:3ff corresponds roughly to MT 9:3ff but with expansions). Notable: LXX 9:2 adds specific reference to the kings gathering "in the region of Gai" (τῆς Γαι). The LXX at v.4 reads ἐπεσιτίσαντο ("they provisioned themselves") rather than the MT's more obscure "disguised themselves," reflecting a different Vorlage or interpretive tradition. EMET follows the MT/Leningrad Codex throughout.

10. **9:1 King lists** -- The Leningrad Codex lists six nations (Hittite, Amorite, Canaanite, Perizzite, Hivite, Jebusite), consistent with the standard hexad. The Girgashite, present in some Deuteronomic lists (Deut 7:1), is absent here.
